attacking_style: Does not typically attack; employs conservative breakaway positioning and tempo control rather than explosive offensive moves.
team_tactics: Prioritizes controlled bunch management with tight gap monitoring (under 90 seconds) and dual-rider breakaway placement to protect sprint or GC assets through defensive racing.
terrain_preference: Stages requiring tempo regulation over explosive power; reduced bunch sprints where climber-sprinters gain advantage through controlled attrition.
vulnerability: Lacks aggressive firepower when race situation demands offensive tactics; relies on circumstances (crashes, breakaway success) rather than forcing outcomes.
key_patterns: Uses breakaways defensively to control tempo rather than seek stage wins (2 instances).
Adjusts objectives opportunistically based on race incidents affecting GC standings (crash-related plan shifts).
Employs precise gap management strategy keeping breaks within narrow margins for sprint setups.
form_trajectory: neutral: Consistent defensive tactical approach across 2021-2022 with focus on marginal gains rather than aggressive racing evolution
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 17
